calendar.matters:
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #2, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Jan-28-2014 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearance: Bruce Feder for Charles Wesley (Administrator).

The Court raised two issues, 1) A 7% holdback and 2) Distribution of Hawaii assets to Albert's heirs. Mr. Feder stated that he has prepared a declaration for distribution of the sale proceeds from the Hawaii property to the four heirs of Albert in accordance with the legal advice provided by Hawaii counsel (Mr. Wong) and in accordance with the Hawaii Probate Code regarding intestate succession and that Hawaii is not a Community Property State. Furthermore, he stated that he is not sure why a 7% tax holdback for out of state beneficiaries but it has been done before. The Court wanted a Declaration with regard to the holdback issue. The matter was taken under submission.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Ella Yip. Bailiff: Deputy Allen. (Not Reported)

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #2, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar May-15-2013 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Bruce Feder for Charles Wesley.

The Court questioned why a creditor's claim was not filed. Mr. Feder explained that his client did not know that a creditor's claim was the proper procedure, and that the beneficiaries have not objected to allowing the reimbursement. The Court suggested that perhaps the beneficiaries should give written consent. Mr. Feder presented further argument, addressed the issue of expenses and stated that all interested parties have had time to make their objections. The accounting petition was taken under submission.

Judge: Lillian K. Sing. Clerk: Nancy Bautista/Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy M. Benton.
